steps.title: Identify the modifier role
steps.content: The sentence’s main subject is “A plano-convex lens,” and its main verb is “ensured.” The words in the blank describe what the lens did to the laser: it focused the laser on the center of the crystal. That descriptive phrase needs a participle, not a second main verb.

steps.title: Eliminate verb-form errors
steps.content: Choice A, “focused,” and Choice B, “focuses,” would create competing main verbs before “ensured.” Choice D, “focus,” does not agree or fit the modifier structure. The participle smoothly modifies “lens” before the main verb “ensured.” The answer is C.
